topic_title: Temperature — low picks the most likely token, high introduces variation
verdict: All checks pass — reading is correctly formatted, on-length (1310 words / ~6.6 min), IBM primary source cited multiple times, no forward references, all 10 sampled assertions trace to corpus, beginner audience maintained throughout, no scope creep (top-p/top-k/beam search absent from body), no assessment references.

format_discipline.notes: First non-empty line is the H1 title. Sidecar parses with word_count=1310, estimated_minutes=7. Exactly 6 sections in correct order.

length.notes: 1310 words / 200 wpm = 6.55 min. Both word count and minutes within target range.

citations.notes: IBM [1] cited at lines 11, 24, 37, 56, 86, 87, 88, 89, 97. [2] cited at lines 51, 56. [3] cited at lines 18, 24, 78, 88. All 3 bibliography entries have corresponding inline citations.

no_forward_references.notes: All concepts used are from prior topics (7.1–7.4) or the 7.5 corpus. 'raw scores' is a new term introduced here but defined inline at point of first use. 'top-p' and 'top-k' appear only in the bibliography citation title for [3], not in body text.

beginner_readability.notes: LLM spelled out on first use (line 4). 'raw scores' defined on first use (line 22). 'softmax' defined inline with parenthetical (line 24). 'stochastic system' and 'deterministic system' both defined inline with parentheticals (line 37). Dial analogy precedes formal definition. No hedging language detected. No paragraph introduces 3+ new terms simultaneously.

formatting.notes: Paragraphs are 2–4 sentences throughout. Worked Example section uses numbered list for the 5-step procedure. Inline examples appear in Key Concepts (dial analogy, e.g. 0.2 and 1.5 examples) and in the distribution table within Key Concepts. Bold-term + plain-meaning pattern used for Temperature, sharp, flat, stochastic system, deterministic system.

assessment_reference.notes: No assessment IDs, due weeks, or grade weights found in reading.md.

scope.notes: No top-p, top-k, nucleus sampling, or beam search content in body. Reference [3] title contains these terms as a bibliographic citation only — not taught or defined. Reading stays within the temperature mechanism boundary.

sample.assertion: Temperature is a number usually between 0 and 2 that controls the probability distribution over the vocabulary
Temperature does not change what the model knows
The model divides every raw score by the temperature value before softmax
Low temperature makes the distribution sharp
High temperature makes the distribution flat
Temperature 0: always pick the top token, no sampling
Customer service chatbots use low temperature around 0.2
Creative writing assistants use higher temperature around 1.0–1.2
Higher temperature does not make the model smarter, only more varied

gold_example_comparison.notes: No gold example file found for reading artifact type. Assessment conducted against written standards in artifact-mix.json and reading-critic SKILL.md.
